name: electronic Data Research and Innovation Service (eDRIS)
description: The electronic Data Research and Innovation Service (eDRIS) is operated by Public Health Scotland and provides access to Scottish health data for research, including hospital episodes, primary care, cancer registrations, prescribing, mortality, and mental health datasets. eDRIS supports researchers through the application process and provides data within the Scottish National Safe Haven.
key_datasets: Scottish Morbidity Records (SMR01 - inpatient, SMR00 - outpatient, SMR04 - mental health)
Mental Health Inpatient and Day Case Dataset (SMR04)
linkages: Community Health Index (CHI) for linkage across Scottish health datasets

requirements.name: PBPP approval
requirements.description: Application reviewed by the Public Benefit and Privacy Panel (Tier 1 or Tier 2 depending on complexity)

requirements.name: PAC approval
requirements.description: Privacy Advisory Committee review for studies accessing Public Health Scotland datasets

requirements.name: Safe Haven access
requirements.description: All data access via the Scottish National Safe Haven secure environment

access_steps.name: eDRIS enquiry and feasibility
access_steps.description: Contact eDRIS to discuss research requirements and data availability within Public Health Scotland holdings

access_steps.name: Submit application
access_steps.description: Complete and submit applications to PBPP and PAC with study protocol, data specification, and legal basis

access_steps.name: PBPP and PAC review
access_steps.description: Dual review by PBPP (public benefit and privacy) and PAC (privacy advisory). Timeline depends on PBPP tier

access_steps.name: Data preparation and Safe Haven provisioning
access_steps.description: eDRIS prepares the approved datasets and provisions workspace within the National Safe Haven

timeline.notes: eDRIS engagement, feasibility, and application preparation
PBPP and PAC dual review process
Data extraction, preparation, and Safe Haven workspace provisioning

costs.notes: No fees for approved research. eDRIS is a publicly funded service within Public Health Scotland.

strengths: Comprehensive Scottish health data from Public Health Scotland
Dedicated support team for researchers throughout the process
Strong linkage across Scottish health datasets via CHI number
limitations: Scotland only - no data from other UK nations
Potentially longest approval times due to dual PBPP and PAC review
Complex application process with multiple governance hurdles
No data extraction - all analysis within Safe Haven
recent_changes: Integration with Research Data Scotland coordination framework
Enhanced Safe Haven platform and analytical tools
Improved researcher support and application guidance
